commands.author: brokemac79

target.title: [Bug]: /pair qr can change gateway.bind and break Tailscale Serve webchat
target.body:
### Bug type

Behavior bug (incorrect output/state without crash)

### Beta release blocker

No

### Summary

Running `/pair qr` from Control UI webchat changed `gateway.bind` to `tailnet`, restarted the Gateway, and left the existing Tailscale Serve root proxy pointing at `127.0.0.1:18789`, causing the webchat URL to return HTTP 502.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Run OpenClaw with Control UI exposed through Tailscale Serve:
   - Tailscale Serve root path proxies to `http://127.0.0.1:18789`
   - Control UI is opened at `https://<ts-host>/chat?session=main`
   - Gateway config initially has `gateway.bind=loopback`
2. In Control UI webchat, run `/pair qr` to pair the iOS app.
3. Wait for the command to progress.
4. Observe Gateway logs detect a `gateway.bind` config change and restart.
5. After restart, observe:
   - Gateway listens only on the tailnet address, e.g. `100.x.x.x:18789`
   - Tailscale Serve still proxies root to `127.0.0.1:18789`
   - `https://<ts-host>/chat?session=main` returns HTTP 502
   - `openclaw health --json` from the host tries `ws://127.0.0.1:18789` and reports a 1006 close while `gateway status --deep` shows the Gateway is otherwise running on the tailnet address

### Expected behavior

`/pair qr` should not silently leave the user's existing Control UI route broken.

If pairing requires changing `gateway.bind`, OpenClaw should either:

- keep loopback reachable,
- update or repair the Tailscale Serve backend,
- choose a compatible QR endpoint without mutating bind, or
- warn before applying a disruptive config change.

After `/pair qr`, the existing Control UI webchat URL should keep loading at `https://<ts-host>/chat?session=main`.

### Actual behavior

The webchat session was disconnected by a service restart, and the normal Tailscale Serve Control UI URL returned HTTP 502 until the Serve backend was manually changed from loopback to the tailnet Gateway address.

Manually restoring `gateway.bind=loopback` once was not durable while the interrupted webchat task was still recoverable: restart recovery resumed the task, it re-applied the `gateway.bind` change, and the Gateway restarted again.

### OpenClaw version

2026.6.10 (aa69b12)

### Operating system

Ubuntu 24.04.4 LTS

### Install method

npm global, systemd user service

### Model

N/A for the failing path. Active agent model was `openai/gpt-5.5`, but this is a Gateway / Control UI / pairing route issue.

### Provider / routing chain

Browser Control UI webchat -> Tailscale Serve HTTPS (`https://<ts-host>`) -> OpenClaw Gateway systemd user service on port `18789`.

### Additional provider/model setup details

Gateway auth token was configured through environment SecretRef. Tailscale Serve was managed outside OpenClaw and already exposed the Control UI root path before `/pair qr` was run.

### Screenshot

Screenshot captured from the reproduced failing state:

![Chrome HTTP 502 for Control UI webchat](https://raw.githubusercontent.com/brokemac79/openclaw/issue-98239-artifacts/issue-artifacts/98239/webchat-502.png)

### Logs, screenshots, and evidence

```shell
# Environment
OpenClaw 2026.6.10 (aa69b12)
Ubuntu 24.04.4 LTS

# Tailscale Serve before workaround
https://<ts-host> (tailnet only)
|-- /           proxy http://127.0.0.1:18789
|-- /agent-wiki proxy http://127.0.0.1:8765

# Config after /pair qr
{
  "gateway": {
    "bind": "tailnet",
    "port": 18789,
    "mode": "local",
    "tailscale": {
      "mode": "off",
      "resetOnExit": false
    },
    "controlUi": {
      "allowedOrigins": [
        "https://<ts-host>",
        "http://<tailnet-ip>:18789"
      ]
    }
  },
  "meta": {
    "lastTouchedAt": "2026-06-30T18:20:17.810Z"
  }
}

# User-visible failure before workaround
curl -k -D - "https://<ts-host>/chat?session=main" -o /dev/null
HTTP/1.1 502 Bad Gateway

# Direct Gateway target was healthy, but loopback backend was gone
curl "http://<tailnet-ip>:18789/chat?session=main" -> HTTP 200
curl "http://127.0.0.1:18789/chat?session=main" -> connection refused

# Gateway logs around first /pair qr run
2026-06-30T18:06:05+00:00 [ws] webchat connected remote=127.0.0.1 client=openclaw-control-ui webchat vcontrol-ui
2026-06-30T18:06:06+00:00 [ws] res chat.startup 206ms
2026-06-30T18:06:12+00:00 [ws] res sessions.create 66ms
2026-06-30T18:08:20+00:00 [reload] config change detected; evaluating reload (meta.lastTouchedAt, gateway.bind)
2026-06-30T18:08:21+00:00 [reload] config change requires gateway restart (gateway.bind) -- deferring until 2 operation(s), 1 reply(ies), 1 embedded run(s) complete
2026-06-30T18:08:37+00:00 systemd: Stopping openclaw-gateway.service - OpenClaw Gateway (v2026.6.10)...
2026-06-30T18:08:37+00:00 [gateway] signal SIGTERM received
2026-06-30T18:08:37+00:00 [shutdown] started: gateway stopping
2026-06-30T18:08:37+00:00 [agent/embedded] codex app-server client closed before turn completed
2026-06-30T18:08:37+00:00 [ws] webchat disconnected code=1012 reason=service restart
2026-06-30T18:08:37+00:00 [shutdown] completed cleanly in 551ms
2026-06-30T18:08:37+00:00 systemd: Started openclaw-gateway.service - OpenClaw Gateway (v2026.6.10).
2026-06-30T18:08:51+00:00 [main-session-restart-recovery] failed to resume interrupted main session agent:main:dashboard:<id>: GatewayTransportError: gateway closed (1006 abnormal closure (no close frame)): no close reason

# After manually restoring gateway.bind=loopback and restarting, the recovered task re-applied the bind change
2026-06-30T18:20:32+00:00 [main-session-restart-recovery] resumed interrupted main session: agent:main:dashboard:<id>
2026-06-30T18:20:32+00:00 [main-session-restart-recovery] main-session restart recovery complete: recovered=1 failed=0 skipped=0
2026-06-30T18:21:44+00:00 [reload] config change detected; evaluating reload (gateway.bind)
2026-06-30T18:21:44+00:00 [reload] config change requires gateway restart (gateway.bind) -- deferring until 2 operation(s), 1 embedded run(s), 1 background task run(s) complete
2026-06-30T18:21:44+00:00 [reload] restart blocked by active background task run(s): taskId=<id> runId=<id> status=running runtime=cli title=[System] Your previous turn was interrupted by a gateway restart while OpenClaw
2026-06-30T18:21:53+00:00 systemd: Stopping openclaw-gateway.service - OpenClaw Gateway (v2026.6.10)...
2026-06-30T18:21:53+00:00 [agent/embedded] codex app-server client closed before turn completed
2026-06-30T18:21:54+00:00 systemd: Started openclaw-gateway.service - OpenClaw Gateway (v2026.6.10).
2026-06-30T18:22:07+00:00 [main-session-restart-recovery] marked interrupted main session failed: agent:main:dashboard:<id> (transcript tail is not resumable)

# Host CLI health after bind changed to tailnet
{
  "ok": false,
  "error": {
    "type": "gateway_transport_error",
    "kind": "closed",
    "message": "gateway closed (1006 abnormal closure (no close frame)): no close reason",
    "code": 1006,
    "reason": "no close reason"
  },
  "gateway": {
    "url": "ws://127.0.0.1:18789",
    "urlSource": "local loopback",
    "bindDetail": "Bind: tailnet"
  }
}
```

### Repeatable route-level repro from the captured broken state

```shell
# Gateway is already in the post-/pair state: gateway.bind=tailnet, listening on <tailnet-ip>:18789.
# Recreate the stale externally managed Tailscale Serve backend that existed before the workaround.
tailscale serve --bg --yes --https=443 http://127.0.0.1:18789

# Observe Control UI/webchat failure through the normal URL.
curl -k -sS -o /dev/null -w 'HTTPS_CHAT=%{http_code}\n' 'https://<ts-host>/chat?session=main'
# HTTPS_CHAT=502

# Observe the Gateway itself is still alive on the tailnet bind.
curl -sS -o /dev/null -w 'TAILNET_CHAT=%{http_code}\n' 'http://<tailnet-ip>:18789/chat?session=main'
# TAILNET_CHAT=200

# Observe the stale Serve backend is no longer valid.
curl -sS -o /dev/null -w 'LOOPBACK_CHAT=%{http_code}\n' 'http://127.0.0.1:18789/chat?session=main'
# curl: (7) Failed to connect to 127.0.0.1 port 18789
```

### Workaround

The immediate workaround is to point Tailscale Serve at the new tailnet Gateway address:

```shell
tailscale serve --bg --yes --https=443 http://<tailnet-ip>:18789
curl -k -sS -o /dev/null -w 'HTTPS_CHAT=%{http_code}\n' 'https://<ts-host>/chat?session=main'
# HTTPS_CHAT=200
```

### Impact and severity

Affected: Users who run `/pair qr` from Control UI webchat on a host where the normal webchat URL is exposed through Tailscale Serve with a loopback backend.

Severity: High for that setup because it breaks the main Control UI/webchat URL with HTTP 502 until the user manually repairs either `gateway.bind` or the Tailscale Serve backend.

Frequency: Observed once for the original `/pair qr` run, then again when the interrupted recovered task re-applied the bind change after manual restoration.

Consequence: User loses the active webchat turn, the webchat page stops opening, and CLI health can report a misleading 1006 loopback transport failure while the Gateway is actually running on the tailnet address.

### Additional information

Related but not the same as #98062 and #98064. Those are iOS app / Tailscale connection and token persistence issues. This report is about the server-side `/pair qr` flow mutating `gateway.bind` without keeping the existing Tailscale Serve Control UI route valid.

I can pick this up and create a PR if maintainers agree the desired behavior is to keep the Control UI route valid when `/pair qr` changes the advertised/bound Gateway address.
